steps.title: Extract temperature values from the scatterplot
steps.content:
Reading the data points directly from the graph:
  • At $x=1$: $y \approx 69°\text{F}$
  • At $x=2$: $y \approx 60°\text{F}$
  • At $x=3$: $y \approx 73°\text{F}$
  • At $x=4$: $y \approx 68°\text{F}$
  • At $x=5$: $y \approx 64°\text{F}$
  • At $x=6$: $y \approx 62°\text{F}$
  • At $x=7$: $y \approx 65°\text{F}$

steps.title: Calculate the temperature change for each interval
steps.content:
For each time period, compute $\Delta y = y_{\text{end}} - y_{\text{start}}$:
  • Choice D ($x=1$ to $x=2$): $60 - 69 = -9°\text{F}$ (decrease)
  • Choice C ($x=2$ to $x=3$): $73 - 60 = 13°\text{F}$ (increase)
  • Choice B ($x=5$ to $x=6$): $62 - 64 = -2°\text{F}$ (decrease)
  • Choice A ($x=6$ to $x=7$): $65 - 62 = 3°\text{F}$ (increase)
The greatest increase is $13°\text{F}$ from day 2 to day 3. That matches choice C — C is correct.
